description: 30-prompt retrieval benchmark for Isaac Assist template retriever. Ground truth verified against workspace/templates/ on 2026-05-15. Thresholds: sim>=0.45 AND margin>=0.20 for hard-instantiate.

prompts.prompt: Build a Franka pick-place cell with a conveyor belt and a single bin
prompts.notes: Canonical single-robot pick-place. Close phrasing to the calibration points. CP-01 should rank clearly above CP-02 (two-robot) if thoughts tokens work.

prompts.prompt: Set up a two-robot assembly line where cubes pass from robot 1 to robot 2 via a second conveyor
prompts.notes: Multi-station two-robot chain. The explicit 'robot 1 to robot 2' phrasing and 'second conveyor' should match CP-02 thoughts tokens over CP-01/CP-51.

prompts.prompt: Sort red and blue cubes into color-matched bins using a Franka arm
prompts.notes: 2-color sort. CP-03 is the canonical; CP-16 (4-color) is also a reasonable alternative. Margin may collapse between the two since their goals are very similar.

prompts.prompt: Create a compact pick-and-place cell that fits within a 2x2 meter footprint
prompts.notes: Footprint constraint is the key discriminator. CP-04 goal mentions '2x2 m footprint' explicitly. Sim tends to be lower due to sparse vocabulary.

prompts.prompt: Build a reorient-and-deliver station where cubes start on their side and a flip-wall tips them upright before pick
prompts.notes: Unique to CP-05. 'flip-wall', 'on its side', 'upright' are very specific discriminating tokens present in CP-05 goal.

prompts.prompt: Franka arm picks cubes from belt and drops into bin, using cuRobo for trajectory planning
prompts.notes: Paraphrase of CP-01 with explicit cuRobo mention. Should retrieve CP-01 or close variants. Alternative phrasing test.

prompts.prompt: UR10 picks colored cubes off a conveyor and routes them to color-matched bins
prompts.notes: UR10-specific color-routing. CP-82 is the two-cube cuRobo version. CP-85 (single-cube builtin) is also valid. The UR10 token is a strong discriminator vs Franka-family.

prompts.prompt: Build a 3-station serial pick-place line with per-station OEE logging
prompts.notes: Very specific. 'OEE', '3-station serial' are unique to CP-NEW-3station-oee. Sim likely above 0.35 but margin may be low.

prompts.prompt: Build a parallel-robot mutual-exclusion pick-place where two Frankas share one conveyor and one bin
prompts.notes: CP-52 has 'parallel-picking duo' + 'setup_robot_claim_mutex' — unique enough to score high. Observed sim ~0.62 in spot checks.

prompts.prompt: Vision-classify cubes on a conveyor and route them to bins based on detected color
prompts.notes: CP-47 is the canonical vision-sorter. CP-48 (inspect-reject) and CP-50 (vision-kitting) are reasonable alternatives.

prompts.prompt: Navigate a Nova Carter AMR to dock at a pick station, load a cube from a Franka arm, then depart
prompts.relabel_note: Relabeled 2026-05-15 — top-1 retrieval is correct; prior label was conservative
prompts.notes: AMR pickup handoff is specific. 'Nova Carter', 'dock', 'depart' are strong discriminating tokens. Baseline top-1=CP-NEW-amr-pickup-handoff, sim=0.571, margin=0.2513.

prompts.prompt: Coordinate 3 AMRs navigating a shared corridor with obstacle avoidance
prompts.relabel_note: Relabeled 2026-05-15 — top-1 retrieval is correct; prior label was conservative
prompts.notes: Multi-AMR corridor navigation. Unique enough that only CP-NEW-multi-amr-corridor should match. Baseline top-1=CP-NEW-multi-amr-corridor, sim=0.6392, margin=0.3448.

prompts.prompt: Train an RL policy with 64 parallel Franka environments using IsaacLab and RSL-RL
prompts.relabel_note: Relabeled 2026-05-15 — top-1 retrieval is correct; prior label was conservative
prompts.notes: RL training with clone_envs. CP-NEW-rl-clone-env has '64 parallel Franka-reach envs cloned via clone_envs'. M-08 (IsaacLab Allegro) is adjacent but different task. Baseline top-1=CP-NEW-rl-clone-env, sim=0.6141, margin=0.2746.

prompts.prompt: Set up ROS2 MoveIt2 bridge for a Franka arm with joint state topics
prompts.notes: CP-87 goal explicitly mentions 'ROS2-MoveIt2 Franka pick-place' and '/isaac_joint_states + /isaac_joint_commands'. Observed sim ~0.43 — may fall below threshold.

prompts.prompt: Stand up 8 Nova Carters each with their own ROS2 namespace
prompts.notes: S-07 goal is 'Stand up 8 Nova Carters with per-robot ROS2 namespaces in one sim'. Very specific phrasing; observed sim ~0.53.

prompts.prompt: Import a robot URDF into Isaac Sim and check its collision meshes
prompts.notes: M-01 is URDF import + collision audit. R-03 is a different prefix but adjacent. 'Import URDF' + 'collision meshes' are strong signals.

prompts.prompt: Anchor the Franka robot base so it doesn't float when simulation starts
prompts.notes: FX-01 is 'Anchor a Franka articulation... so its base does not free-fall on Play'. 'anchor', 'float', 'simulation' are discriminating. Observed sim ~0.28-0.34.

prompts.prompt: Generate 10k COCO-format synthetic images for apple detection with stable annotation IDs
prompts.notes: P-01 goal is 'Generate 10k COCO apple-detection images where annotation IDs stay stable'. Very specific — 'COCO', 'apple', 'annotation IDs' distinguish from CP-NEW-defect-sdg.

prompts.prompt: Build an IsaacLab task from scratch: Allegro hand with cube reorientation, InteractiveSceneCfg, no GUI
prompts.relabel_note: Relabeled 2026-05-15 — top-1 retrieval is correct; prior label was conservative
prompts.notes: M-08 goal matches verbatim on 'Allegro hand', 'InteractiveSceneCfg', 'no GUI'. High specificity. Baseline top-1=M-08, sim=0.6793, margin=0.2096.

prompts.prompt: Set up a Carter wheeled robot for autonomous navigation using Nav2
prompts.notes: CP-64 is 'mobile robot with Nav2 navigation config: a Carter wheeled robot via setup_nav_robot'. Very specific. Observed sim ~0.64.

prompts.prompt: pick and place
prompts.notes: Too generic. No template should fire hard-instantiate. Expected: sim < 0.45 for all top-K.

prompts.prompt: Franka and conveyor
prompts.notes: Ambiguous: both CP-01 and CP-02 are plausible. Expected: low margin (< 0.20) even if sim is moderate, so few-shot fallback fires.

prompts.prompt: sort items into bins
prompts.notes: Vague sort request. Missing color/count/robot info. Expected: sim < 0.40 or margin < 0.20 for all candidates.

prompts.prompt: I need help with Isaac Sim
prompts.notes: Completely generic help request. Expected: sim near 0 for all templates. Graceful no-match.

prompts.prompt: my robot isn't moving what's wrong
prompts.notes: Diagnostic question, no build intent. Expected: low sim across board. AD-series templates exist but are unlikely to surface with confidence.

prompts.prompt: palletize boxes
prompts.notes: Ambiguous — many palletizer templates exist (2x2, 3x3, 4x4, 6-cube, brick-layer). No single canonical should dominate with margin >= 0.20. Expected: few-shot fallback is correct.

prompts.prompt: build a contact-rich peg insertion task with force sensing and 5mm clearance
prompts.notes: CP-58 is the peg-in-hole array canonical. CP-NEW-peg-in-hole-single is the single-peg variant. 'force sensing', '5mm clearance' are discriminating. Expect sim ~0.38.

prompts.prompt: OPC-UA bridge connecting 12 conveyors to external PLC
prompts.notes: CP-NEW-opcua-12conveyors is very specific. 'OPC-UA', '12 conveyors', 'PLC' are strong tokens. Observed sim ~0.39.

prompts.prompt: UR5e arm picks cubes off a stationary feeder platform and fills a bin
prompts.notes: UR5e is listed in CP-01 roles.primary_robot.constraints. The stationary feeder pattern matches CP-75 (UR10 static-cube) too. Tests robot-name aliasing — UR5e is not the primary training phrasing.

prompts.prompt: drawer opening station: robot pulls open a cabinet drawer using a prismatic joint
prompts.notes: CP-55 is the drawer-open station canonical. CP-NEW-drawer-open is the draft variant. 'prismatic joint', 'cabinet drawer' are strong discriminators. Observed sim ~0.59.
